commands.review_summary:
The branch adds GIT_ALLOW_PROTOCOL and GIT_PROTOCOL_FROM_USER blocking/filtering to the shared host-env policy, generated macOS policy/sanitizer, and focused TypeScript regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +43, Tests +235, Generated +4, Other +41. Total +323 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and dependency contract, though I did not run a local repro because this review is read-only. Current main does not block these two env vars, and Git documents them as protocol policy controls that can allow or deny transports.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual maintainer review is needed for the protected maintainer label, the security/compatibility policy acceptance, and the contributor real behavior proof gate.

target.title: fix: block git protocol env controls [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Blocks request-scoped Git protocol-control environment variables in the shared host environment security policy.
- Filters inherited `GIT_ALLOW_PROTOCOL` to Git safe default protocols and preserves inherited `GIT_PROTOCOL_FROM_USER` only when keeping it does not make Git more permissive, including false-like and malformed fail-closed values.
- Keeps the macOS sanitizer and generated policy in parity with the shared TypeScript policy.
- Adds focused sanitizer coverage plus live Git regressions for `ext::` helper execution and restrictive allowlist non-widening.
- AI-assisted.

Out of scope: broader host environment policy reshaping, config changes, and unrelated Git transport policy changes.

## Linked context

Security maintainer-requested host environment hardening. Private tracking context intentionally omitted from the PR body.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Git protocol-control environment variables are blocked or filtered before host execution so inherited or request-scoped env cannot loosen Git transport policy.
- Real environment tested: Local source checkout on Linux with system Git, Node/Vitest wrapper, core test-type lane, and local OpenGrep changed-path scan.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/host-env-security.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.reported-baseline.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.policy-parity.test.ts`; `node scripts/generate-host-env-security-policy-swift.mjs --check`; `scripts/run-opengrep.sh --changed --json`; `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix: 3 Vitest files passed, 40 tests passed; generated Swift policy check passed; local changed-path OpenGrep reported 0 findings; GitHub PR checks passed on the final head.
- Observed result after fix: Regression tests prove unsafe env can execute a Git `ext::` helper before sanitization, while sanitized env blocks that path; inherited allowlists are filtered without widening local file transport access; inherited fail-closed `GIT_PROTOCOL_FROM_USER` values are preserved.
- What was not tested: Full cross-platform package/release packaging.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: No live channel/provider credentials were used; this is a host-exec sanitizer boundary.
- Before evidence: The prior policy did not include the two Git protocol-control variables in the blocked key set.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/host-env-security.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.reported-baseline.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.policy-parity.test.ts`
- `node scripts/generate-host-env-security-policy-swift.mjs --check`
- `scripts/run-opengrep.sh --changed --json`
- `git diff --check`
- Review-pr: passed on final head with zero findings.
- Autoreview: passed on final head with no accepted/actionable findings.
- GitHub PR checks: passed on final head.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`): Yes, host execution now blocks or filters two additional Git-related environment variables.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`): Yes, host tool execution environment sanitization is stricter for Git transport policy controls.

What is the highest-risk area?

Operators who intentionally relied on passing permissive Git protocol-control variables into OpenClaw-spawned host Git commands will no longer get that behavior.

How is that risk mitigated?

The blocked variables directly alter Git transport policy. The change lands in the existing host-env security policy boundary, keeps generated macOS parity, preserves inherited fail-closed restrictions, and adds focused regression coverage.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review/merge.

Waiting on: None known.

Bot or reviewer comments addressed: Review-pr and autoreview findings were addressed; final review-pr and autoreview are clean.
